section_title: When police may arrest without warrant
full_text: Any police officer may without an order from a Magistrate and without a warrant, arrest any person who has been concerned in any cognizable offence...
plain_language: Police can arrest you without a warrant only for serious crimes or if they have reasonable suspicion.

section_title: Notice of appearance before police officer
full_text: The police officer shall, in all cases where the arrest of a person is not required under the provisions of sub-section (1) of section 41, issue a notice directing the person against whom a reasonable complaint has been made...
plain_language: If arrest is not necessary, police must first issue a notice for you to appear before them instead of arresting you immediately.

section_title: Procedure of arrest and duties of officer making arrest
full_text: Every police officer while making an arrest shall bear an accurate, visible and clear identification of his name which will facilitate easy identification; prepare a memorandum of arrest which shall be attested by at least one witness...
plain_language: The arresting officer must wear a clear name tag, prepare an arrest memo, and get it signed by a witness and you.

section_title: Right of arrested person to meet an advocate of his choice during interrogation
full_text: When any person is arrested and interrogated by the police, he shall be entitled to meet an advocate of his choice during interrogation, though not throughout interrogation.
plain_language: You have the right to meet and talk to your lawyer during police questioning.

full_text: In making an arrest the police officer or other person making the same shall actually touch or confine the body of the person to be arrested, unless there be a submission to the custody by word or action...
plain_language: Police should not use more force than necessary to arrest you. Women can generally only be arrested by female officers and after sunrise/before sunset.

section_title: Person arrested to be informed of grounds of arrest and of right to bail
full_text: Every police officer or other person arresting any person without warrant shall forthwith communicate to him full particulars of the offence for which he is arrested or other grounds for such arrest.
plain_language: Police must immediately tell you exactly why you are being arrested and if you have a right to bail.

section_title: Person arrested to be taken before Magistrate or officer in charge of police station
full_text: A police officer making an arrest without warrant shall, without unnecessary delay and subject to the provisions herein contained as to bail, take or send the person arrested before a Magistrate having jurisdiction in the case...
plain_language: Police must take you to a Magistrate or a senior police officer without any unnecessary delay after arrest.

section_title: Person arrested not to be detained more than twenty-four hours
full_text: No police officer shall detain in custody a person arrested without warrant for a longer period than under all the circumstances of the case is reasonable, and such period shall not, in the absence of a special order of a Magistrate under section 167, exceed twenty-four hours exclusive of the time necessary for the journey from the place of arrest to the Magistrate's Court.
plain_language: Police cannot keep you in custody for more than 24 hours without taking you before a Magistrate.

section_title: Protection in respect of conviction for offences
full_text: No person shall be convicted of any offence except for violation of a law in force at the time of the commission of the act... No person shall be prosecuted and punished for the same offence more than once...
plain_language: You cannot be punished for something that wasn't a crime when you did it, and you cannot be punished twice for the same crime.

section_title: Protection of life and personal liberty
full_text: No person shall be deprived of his life or personal liberty except according to procedure established by law.
plain_language: Your life and freedom are protected. You can only be arrested if the police follow the correct legal process.

section_title: Protection against arrest and detention in certain cases
full_text: No person who is arrested shall be detained in custody without being informed, as soon as may be, of the grounds for such arrest nor shall he be denied the right to consult, and to be defended by, a legal practitioner of his choice.
plain_language: You must be told why you are arrested and you have the right to talk to and be defended by a lawyer.

act_name: Supreme Court Guidelines (D.K. Basu v. State of West Bengal)
full_text: The Supreme Court laid down 11 mandatory guidelines for arrest, including: clear identification of officers, preparation of arrest memo, right to inform a relative, entries in diary, medical examination...
plain_language: The Supreme Court has set strict rules for police during arrest: they must identify themselves, let you inform your family, and record everything in a diary.

section_title: Arrest procedures and duties of officer making arrest
full_text: New provisions replacing CrPC Section 41 series, emphasizing digital records and rights of the arrested person...
plain_language: Updated laws (from 2024) continue to protect you during arrest, requiring police to follow specific procedures and inform you of your rights.

full_text: Consolidated rights of arrested persons under the new criminal code...
plain_language: The new law ensures you are informed of your right to bail and grounds of arrest immediately.

section_title: Obligation of person making arrest to inform about the arrest, etc., to a nominated person
full_text: Every police officer or other person making any arrest under this Code shall forthwith give the information regarding such arrest and place where the arrested person is being held to any of his friends, relatives or such other persons as may be disclosed or nominated by the arrested person...
plain_language: Police must immediately inform a friend or relative of your choice about your arrest and where you are being held.
